agent_notes: Redbridge produced a useful but lean PAINTS list: Wanstead is strongest for drinks and markets, Ilford has the clearest gallery/museum spine, and Hainault/Valentines are the only Creative Walk green spaces found that appear on the PAINTS v6 approved safety list.

No Books & Coffee venue was included because I could not verify an independent Redbridge bookshop with an on-site or literally-next-door coffee offer, 2-hour seating, and group walk-in tolerance above the PAINTS score floor.
Host must still confirm Google ratings/review counts, live walk-in tolerance for groups of 8-12, bank-holiday hours, and private-hire conflicts before publishing.

venues.opening_hours: Official booking page lists Wanstead: Monday closed; Tuesday 17:00-22:00; Wednesday 17:00-22:30; Thursday 17:00-22:30; Friday 15:00-23:00; Saturday 12:00-23:00; Sunday closed except first Sunday of month 12:00-19:00. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm bank holiday/private-event exceptions.
venues.walk_in_policy: Bookings are offered; walk-ins for 8-12 are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Treat as early-arrival or pre-call only.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Likely suitable for 8-10 at off-peak wine-bar hours; 12 should be confirmed directly because private tastings and tasting lounges may affect availability.
venues.capacity_notes: Small independent wine-bar format with seated food/wine service and tasting programme; exact public capacity not published.
venues.why_fits_paints: A proper wine-led room on a village high street gives Redbridge a more intimate alternative to pub-only drinks. The 70-plus wines by the glass and tasting programme create natural show-and-tell hooks without needing a formal class.
venues.anecdote_story: MUST positions itself around wine education and tastings rather than a generic bar offer, with customer copy on its own site naming staff knowledge and tasting evenings as part of the experience.
venues.serve_or_on_view: 70+ wines by the glass from 20 countries, food, private tasting lounges and monthly wine tasting programme.
venues.safety_notes: Wanstead High Street is a well-lit village centre with Central line access; route attendees via Wanstead or Snaresbrook stations and avoid late dispersal through quieter residential streets.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 7-9 min walk via High Street/Nightingale Lane - host should verify exact Google walking route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Group walk-in status is the main operational risk; if MUST requires booking for 8-12, keep Tier A only if PAINTS holds the booking, otherwise demote to Tier B.

venues.opening_hours: Official site describes lunch, dinner, Perky Blenders coffee, Sunday roasts and regular music/DJ programming, but full weekly hours were not visible in search snippet. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm exact hours, bank-holiday handling and private events.
venues.walk_in_policy: Instagram states walk-ins are always welcome in at least one recent context, while the site also pushes bookings; group of 8-12 walk-in tolerance is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Better suited than a micropub for 8-12, but likely needs off-peak timing or a heads-up call.
venues.capacity_notes: Neighbourhood pub and dining room with private-dining/event signals; exact walk-in seating allocation not published.
venues.best_time_slot: Tuesday-Thursday 18:00-20:00 or Sunday first live-music afternoon if host confirms suitability.
venues.why_fits_paints: The Duke is a lived-in neighbourhood pub rather than a novelty bar, with enough programme texture for strangers to talk around. It is less wine-specialist than MUST but has stronger group resilience.
venues.anecdote_story: OpenTable/SquareMeal copy identifies it as a neighbourhood pub off the high street and notes it was voted Time Out's favourite pub for Leyton, Leytonstone and Wanstead in 2017.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Pub drinks, lunch/dinner, Sunday roasts, Perky Blenders coffee, DJs and live music.
venues.safety_notes: Nightingale Lane is residential but close to Wanstead High Street; end before late-night quiet hours and route back via lit main streets.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 7-9 min walk via High Street/Nightingale Lane - host should verify exact Google walking route.
venues.risks_watchouts: It is more gastro-pub than wine/craft-led, so keep as Tier B unless the event angle is local-pub storytelling rather than natural-wine discovery.

venues.opening_hours: Ace Events lists Wanstead Market on High Street E11 2AE, first Sunday monthly, 10:00-16:00. Redbridge licensing material lists 2026 market dates including 5 July, 2 August, 6 September, 4 October, 1 November and 6/19 December, with sale 10:00-16:00. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm weather/cancellation notices close to date.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; no individual booking required. Meeting-point walk-in at Bare Brew is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm for 8-12.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for a roaming 8-12 group if hosted as staggered browsing rather than everyone queuing at one stall.
venues.capacity_notes: Licensing bulletin describes up to 35 stalls selling foods, crafted goods, plants and flowers.
venues.best_time_slot: First Sunday of the month, 10:30-12:30 before late lunch crowding.
venues.why_fits_paints: This is the most PAINTS-coded Redbridge market: local food, crafts, plants and a village high-street setting with independent cafes nearby. It gives a group a low-pressure sequence of micro-discoveries.
venues.anecdote_story: Council material frames Wanstead High Street as having wide pavements, village appeal and independent shops/cafes; the monthly market reinforces that civic identity.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Farmers market stalls with seasonal produce, prepared food, bakery, cheese, plants, flowers, crafts and jewellery.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime high-street event with strong footfall and Central line access; avoid using nearby Wanstead Flats as a meeting or walk segment because it is explicitly removed from the PAINTS Creative Walk list.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10-15 min by Central line or bus; market backup radius allows 15 min walk or transit. Host should verify live route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Monthly-only cadence and weather exposure; host must verify the specific date has not been cancelled or displaced.

venues.opening_hours: Ace Events lists South Woodford Market on George Lane E18 1AY, third Sunday monthly, 10:00-16:00. Redbridge licensing material also lists 2026 dates with market sale 11:00-17:00 in one bulletin; exact current public trading hours must be confirmed with Ace Events/Redbridge.
venues.walk_in_policy: Open-access market; no individual booking required. Meeting-point cafe for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for an 8-12 hosted browse if the meeting point is kept outside the main stall pinch-points.
venues.capacity_notes: Redbridge licensing bulletin describes a monthly market of up to 20 stalls selling food, crafted goods, plants and flowers.
venues.best_time_slot: Third Sunday of the month, 10:30-12:30 if Ace confirms a 10:00 start; otherwise 11:15-13:15.
venues.why_fits_paints: Smaller and less destination-heavy than Wanstead, but still has a clear local-producer/community-market shape. The George Lane high street makes it practical for low-cost Sunday social browsing.
venues.anecdote_story: The market is part of Redbridge's official street-trading programme for Wanstead and South Woodford, designed to complement existing local shops.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Food, craft, plants and flowers; exact stall mix changes monthly.
venues.safety_notes: Daytime high-street setting near South Woodford station; use George Lane and station approaches for arrivals/departures.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 10-15 min by Central line or bus; market backup radius allows 15 min walk or transit. Host should verify live route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Public sources conflict on 10:00-16:00 vs 11:00-17:00; host must verify live hours and stall count before scheduling.

venues.opening_hours: SPACE visit page lists gallery opening during exhibitions Wednesday 11:00-17:00 and Saturday 11:00-15:00, and by appointment. Current/near-current programme lists Sabrina Tirvengadum, Beauty Fall, 29 April-10 October 2026, free and all welcome. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm event-date availability.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free gallery drop-in signals and 'free and all welcome' exhibition copy suggest walk-ins; group of 8-12 and guided-tour capacity are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 if the host staggers viewing and confirms no workshop/private event conflict.
venues.capacity_notes: Artist studios, gallery and event space at rear of Redbridge Town Hall; exact gallery capacity not published.
venues.why_fits_paints: SPACE is the borough's strongest contemporary-art anchor and has an unusually local civic setting behind Redbridge Town Hall. The current programme is participatory, free and grounded in Redbridge residents' stories rather than a generic white-cube show.
venues.anecdote_story: SPACE opened Ilford studios and a gallery in 2019 at the rear of Grade II listed Redbridge Town Hall, creating affordable artist workspace and a public gallery inside a civic landmark.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Current 2026 programme includes Sabrina Tirvengadum's Beauty Fall and related tours/workshops; earlier 2026 programme included Tadhg Charles, Ley Lines, made with Redbridge residents through storytelling, 3D scanning, sound and AI.
venues.safety_notes: Town-centre daytime route beside Redbridge Central Library and Town Hall; use Ilford station/Clements Road and avoid late evening starts because regular public opening is daytime.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 1-2 min walk across Clements Road/Oakfield Road - host should verify exact accessible route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Public opening is only Wednesday/Saturday during exhibitions; host must check the exhibition install/deinstall calendar and whether 8-12 can enter together.

venues.opening_hours: Redbridge Council lists Tuesday-Saturday 11:00-17:00. Vision RCL lists the museum Tuesday-Saturday 11:00-17:00 and says the brand-new permanent exhibition reopened in August 2024. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm bank-holiday handling.
venues.walk_in_policy: Free civic museum format suggests walk-in, but group of 8-12 tolerance and any school/private use conflict are UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Suitable for a compact hosted museum visit if the group avoids school-visit peaks and confirms with museum staff.
venues.capacity_notes: Located on the 2nd floor of Redbridge Central Library; exact exhibition-room capacity not published.
venues.why_fits_paints: It is not a blockbuster art show, but it gives Redbridge-specific story depth: migration, local identity, wartime history and civic memory. It works best paired with SPACE rather than as a standalone art-led day.
venues.anecdote_story: The museum covers more than 150,000 years of local history and reopened a redeveloped permanent exhibition in August 2024.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Permanent local-history exhibition; Vision RCL what's-on lists trails and displays including Super Science/STEM, Memories of Windrush, Redbridge and the Second World War, and Discover Sylvia Pankhurst.
venues.safety_notes: Central Library/Town Hall civic quarter is appropriate for daytime arrivals; use Ilford station and main streets.
venues.backup_walk_time: About 1-2 min walk across Clements Road/Oakfield Road - host should verify exact accessible route.
venues.risks_watchouts: Capped at Tier B because I did not find a current exhibition press citation within the last 3 months that would itself justify a PAINTS Museum & Gallery Day.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Valentines Park / Gants Hill or Ilford stations
venues.opening_hours: Park is open-access in daytime; Redbridge/Vision describe Valentines Park as the borough's premiere award-winning park and note cafes in the park. Valentines Mansion/Friends pages list mansion open Sundays and Mondays, March-October 12:00-17:00 and November-February 11:00-15:00. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm park gate times, cafe hours and event-date mansion access.
venues.walk_in_policy: Park is open-access; mansion/gallery access is free on open days but group tour access is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm. Weather backup at Valentines Park Cafe is walk-in UNVERIFIED - host must confirm for 8-12.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Strong for 8-12 as a hosted daytime route, with pauses at the mansion exterior/gardens, Long Water/walled garden, boating lake/bandstand and historic park landscape.
venues.capacity_notes: Large public park; indoor mansion access is limited to public open days and may not fit a group without confirmation.
venues.best_time_slot: Sunday 12:30-14:30 when the mansion is normally open, or Saturday late morning for park-only route with cafe backup.
venues.why_fits_paints: Valentines Park is explicitly on the PAINTS Safety-First shortlist and has the strongest Redbridge mix of designed landscape, mansion history and conversational stops. It feels outer-London specific rather than a generic park walk.
venues.anecdote_story: The park began as the grounds of Valentines Mansion, built around 1696/97; London Gardens Trust notes the estate's Long Pond, wilderness, ha-ha and the famed Valentines Vine, whose cutting went to Hampton Court Palace.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Suggested stops: Valentines Mansion exterior and restored gardens; Long Water/walled garden; boating lake and bandstand; historic cricket/pavilion landscape.
venues.safety_notes: Creative Walk approved because Valentines Park appears on the PAINTS v6 Safety-First Shortlist. Use daytime only, keep to main paths, and finish before dusk.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within about 5 min of central park meeting points; host should verify exact route from chosen start.
venues.risks_watchouts: Mansion is only open two days weekly; if the event requires indoor access, schedule Sunday/Monday and confirm no private hire.

venues.neighbourhood_station: Hainault Forest / Hainault or Fairlop stations plus bus
venues.opening_hours: Hainault Forest official/Christmas hours pages show forest daytime opening and 1856 Cafe seasonal hours, but standard 2026 park/cafe hours were not fully verified in public snippets. UNVERIFIED - host must confirm gate, cafe, farm and visitor centre hours for the date.
venues.walk_in_policy: Forest is open-access during park hours; 1856 Cafe walk-in for 8-12 is UNVERIFIED - host must confirm.
venues.group_suitability_8_12: Good for 8-12 in daylight if the host keeps the route short, starts/ends at the visitor centre and avoids remote woodland sections.
venues.capacity_notes: Large ancient-woodland/country-park setting with visitor centre, cafe, lake and farm facilities; public transport access is weaker than Wanstead/Ilford.
venues.best_time_slot: Wednesday or weekend 10:30-12:30, starting at the visitor centre/cafe.
venues.why_fits_paints: Hainault Forest is on the PAINTS Safety-First shortlist and has a restored visitor hub, cafe and ancient-woodland story that can support a proper creative walk. It is demoted to Tier B because transport and backup resilience are weaker than Valentines Park.
venues.anecdote_story: Vision RCL describes a £7m restoration with reconstructed Victorian barns, new visitor hub, cafe, adventure play, and artist/business workshops; Woodland Trust frames Hainault as ancient wooded landscape connected to a country park.
venues.serve_or_on_view: Suggested stops: Woodland Trust visitor centre exhibits/map table; restored 1856 barn/cafe; lake edge; short woodland trail/grassland viewpoint.
venues.safety_notes: Creative Walk approved because Hainault Forest Country Park appears on the PAINTS v6 Safety-First Shortlist. Daytime only; avoid dusk, poor weather and remote woodland detours.
venues.backup_walk_time: Within 5 min of visitor-centre start if the walk starts at the cafe/visitor hub.
venues.risks_watchouts: Outer location, seasonal hours, and limited public-transport options; host must check weather, cafe capacity and safe return route before listing.

rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Wanstead Flats is explicitly listed as removed in the PAINTS v6 park safety list, and Wanstead Park is not on the approved Premium or Safety-First lists. Do not source as a Creative Walk even though it is locally attractive.

rejected_candidates.venue_name: Exchange Ilford / mall-led food and coffee options
rejected_candidates.reason_rejected: Shopping-centre setting and chain/templated retail mix fail the PAINTS aesthetic filter; no qualifying independent bookshop-cafe proposition found.
Useful bookshop but no verified on-site or literally-next-door independent coffee offer, 2-hour seating, or group walk-in tolerance; below PAINTS Books & Coffee threshold.

Operationally robust pub with central location, but the public evidence reads more mainstream pub/food/sports than PAINTS-specific drinks culture; weaker owner/story/aesthetic signal than MUST or The Duke.

Excellent heritage asset, but public sources did not provide a current exhibition with recent press strong enough for PAINTS Museum & Gallery Days. Folded into the approved Valentines Park Creative Walk instead.
Theatre is culturally important but not a qualifying museum/gallery day under PAINTS v6; auditorium is also temporarily closed due to RAAC precautions, with activity moved to foyers/other spaces.
